title: Maximum Number of Locked Entities
text: Maximum Number of Locked Entities The maximum number of files or tables which can be open at one time depends on the number of I/O channels the DBMS connector requires and the setting for $MAXFILES in an assignment file. Under almost all circumstances, you do not need to be concerned with this restriction because Uniface ensures that files are closed and opened as necessary. However, because a locked file cannot be closed, this maximum does determine how many files can be locked at the same time. The formula to determine the maximum number of simultaneously locked files is as follows: ($MAXFILES - 5) / I/O_ChannelsNeeded That is, subtract five from $MAX_FILES and divide it by the number of I/O channels required.
